lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you hunting animals, then?

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: I'm exploring the region for the good of the gnome people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not tracking down huge beasts to kill - you look equipped for it.

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: Well, if I find anything interesting I'll try and bring it back to the King. That might include animals, I suppose. I'd rather not kill it, though - most animals are more useful alive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How would you get it home, then?

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: Never underestimate the ingenuity of a gnome, friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you found anything else interesting around here?

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: I've heard strange rumours about something the locals call the Broodoo. I haven't quite worked out what they are but they seem to be some sort of undead whose ghastly fate depends on the way they were killed.

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: I think they may be sacrifice victims - but I'm not sure why they are sacrificed. Either way the locals seem to be very scared of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's interesting. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does this mint cake do?

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: Gnomish mint cake is a great source of energy for the gnome traveller. It will give you that extra bit of zip when you are running out of steam.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ooh, that might come in very handy. Do you have any spare?

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: I need all the mint cake I have, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does this seed pod do?

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: Ah, the seed pod of the Grand Tree is a fascinating spirit artefact. It is a very useful thing, though one must treat it with respect, since the Grand Tree has a connection with all of its parts, however distant they

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: may be. How do you think the Spirit Trees work?

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: If you launch its seeds in a far flung location, you will be helping the tree survive, so the grand tree will help a gnome glider pilot find you, using the seed burst itself (which marks the sky) and a little magic, and bring you

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: back home as an act of gratitude. Budding farmers will also find their link with plants to be just a little bit better than before.

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: However, it takes time for a pilot to find you, and if you are in a real hurry, and I mean a real emergency, you can just squash the seed pod. The Grand Tree doesn't like this, but will pull you back

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: home straight away. Don't do this if you need to do anything with plants straight away, though - your fingers will be considerably less green for a while after using the seed pod this way, and the journey is not

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: very pleasant...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, that's very interesting. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: At last a taste of home!

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: Now that I have some proper food, I won't need this any more.

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: Here, take these Grand Seed pods. Very useful in emergencies!

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: I traded with some of the locals for this - maybe you'll find it useful.

lines.speaker: Penwie
lines.text: You must have had to travel a long way to get this to me. Here's a little something extra as a tip.
